# Canopy

> A modern C++ RPC library with IDL-generated proxy/stub code, multiple transport
> layers, optional coroutine support, and SGX enclave builds.

## What Canopy is

Canopy lets you define service interfaces in a small IDL language and get generated
C++ proxy/stub pairs. The same interface works in-process, across processes via
shared memory or TCP, or across trust boundaries in SGX enclaves — without changing
the interface definition or calling code.

## Using Canopy in your own project (add_subdirectory pattern)

The recommended way to consume Canopy is `add_subdirectory`. No system install is
needed. See:

- [`documents/external-project-guide.md`](documents/external-project-guide.md) —
  complete walkthrough: CMake layout, IDL syntax, TCP server/client patterns, known
  pitfalls. **Start here.**
- [`test_app/`](../test_app/) — a working two-process example (server + client over
  TCP) that uses this exact pattern.

Quick CMakeLists.txt sketch:

```cmake
set(CANOPY_BUILD_COROUTINE ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)  # needed for TCP
set(CANOPY_BUILD_TEST OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
add_subdirectory(../Canopy canopy_build)
add_subdirectory(idl)                               # calls CanopyGenerate()
target_link_libraries(my_exe PRIVATE my_service_idl transport_streaming streaming_tcp rpc canopy_network_config ${CANOPY_LIBRARIES})
```

## Repository layout

- `rpc/` — core library; public headers under `rpc/include/rpc`
- `generator/` — IDL compiler; produces proxy/stub C++ from `.idl` files
- `transports/` — local, streaming (TCP/SPSC), SGX, dynamic-library transports
- `streaming/` — coroutine streaming stack (TCP, TLS, WebSocket, io_uring)
- `subcomponents/network_config/` — CLI arg parsing for host/port/routing-prefix
- `cmake/` — CMake modules including `CanopyGenerate.cmake`
- `documents/` — background guides (not authoritative; verify from source)
- `demos/` — working example programs; `demos/comprehensive/` covers all transports

## Build system

- CMake ≥ 3.24, Ninja, clang/clang++ by default
- C++17 baseline; C++20 with `CANOPY_BUILD_COROUTINE=ON`
- Presets defined in `CMakePresets.json`; common ones: `Debug`, `Debug_Coroutine`, `Release`
- `BUILD_TYPE` custom variable has been removed; use `CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE` only

## IDL quick reference

```idl
namespace my_app
{
    interface i_my_service
    {
        int my_method([in] const std::string& input, [out] std::string& output);
        int add(int a, int b, [out] int& result);
    };
}
```

`CanopyGenerate(my_service my_service/my_service.idl ...)` produces the
`my_service_idl` CMake target. Include `<my_service/my_service.h>` in your sources.

## Coroutine macros

`CORO_TASK(T)`, `CO_RETURN`, `CO_AWAIT` — compatibility macros that expand to
coroutine primitives when `CANOPY_BUILD_COROUTINE=ON` and to blocking equivalents
otherwise.

## Agent / LLM instructions

See [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) for working practices, build commands, pointer/ownership
rules, and session-completion checklist used by AI coding assistants.
